mission.title: Install and Configure Piraeus Datastore on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Piraeus Operator manages LINSTOR clusters in Kubernetes, providing a robust solution for software-defined storage. Installing it allows you to easily deploy and manage storage resources in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for Piraeus
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for the Piraeus Operator to run in. This helps in organizing resources and managing access controls.

steps.title: Deploy the Piraeus Operator
steps.description: Apply the Piraeus Operator manifests to your Kubernetes cluster. This will set up the necessary resources for the operator.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check that the Piraeus Operator pods are running correctly in the 'piraeus' namespace.

uninstall.title: Remove the Piraeus Operator
uninstall.description: To uninstall the Piraeus Operator, delete the resources created in the 'piraeus' namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Piraeus Operator
upgrade.description: To upgrade the Piraeus Operator, apply the new version of the manifests. Make sure to specify the correct version.

troubleshooting.title: Pods Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Piraeus Operator pods are not starting, check the events in the namespace for errors using: 
```bash
kubectl get events -n piraeus
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If you receive errors related to insufficient resources, ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated for the Piraeus Operator.

troubleshooting.title: Network Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter network issues, verify that your Kubernetes network policies allow communication between the Piraeus Operator and LINSTOR components.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of the Piraeus Operator will have the operator pod running in the 'piraeus-datastore' namespace, and you will be able to create a LINSTOR cluster. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring no errors are present in the logs.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
